[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by exploiting NodeMap keys containing malformed IP:port strings (no format validation in the type definition), causing the Hiero SDK to fail when attempting to establish connections to nodes, resulting in transaction submission failures for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: Db] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database host redirection by exploiting Db.Host being an unvalidated string that accepts hostnames with embedded credentials (e.g., 'user:pass@realhost'), causing the PostgreSQL driver to parse the host field as a URL and use attacker-supplied credentials instead of the configured ones?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: Pool] Can an unprivileged external user cause a connection pool exhaustion by exploiting Pool.MaxOpenConnections being set to a very large value (no upper bound), combined with a request flood, causing the service to open thousands of database connections simultaneously and exhaust the PostgreSQL server's connection limit, denying service to all users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: Cache] Can an unprivileged external user cause a cache poisoning denial-of-service by exploiting Cache.MaxSize being set to 1 (no minimum validation above 1), causing the entity cache to evict entries on every request and provide no caching benefit, amplifying database load and causing service degradation under normal traffic?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: Config] Can an unprivileged external user cause a goroutine leak by exploiting Config.NodeRefreshInterval being set to a very large duration, causing the node refresh goroutine to block for an extremely long time, and if the service receives a shutdown signal during this wait, the goroutine may not be properly cancelled, leading to a goroutine leak?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: Config] Can an unprivileged external user cause a service hang during shutdown by exploiting Config.ShutdownTimeout being set to an extremely large duration (no upper bound validation), causing the graceful shutdown to wait for an unreasonably long time for in-flight requests to complete, effectively preventing the service from restarting after a crash?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: CommonConfig] Can an unprivileged external user cause a zero-value realm confusion by exploiting CommonConfig.Realm defaulting to zero (int64 zero value) when not explicitly configured, causing entity IDs to be constructed with realm=0 regardless of the actual network realm, leading to incorrect entity resolution for networks with non-zero realms?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Function: GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a connection downgrade attack by exploiting the hardcoded sslmode=disable in GetDsn(), where even if the PostgreSQL server is configured to require SSL, the client-side disable overrides server requirements in some driver configurations, allowing unencrypted connections that expose all database traffic?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: '] [Type: Db] Can an unprivileged external user cause a password exposure via process listing by exploiting the fact that GetDsn() embeds the password in the DSN string, and if this DSN is passed as a command-line argument or environment variable to a subprocess, it becomes visible in /proc/<pid>/cmdline or /proc/<pid>/environ to any local user?
